report_name: Financial Services Sector Faces Double the Cyberattacks of Other Industries, SonicWall Reports

threat_actors.name: REvil
threat_actors.description: A notorious ransomware group actively targeting the financial services sector by exploiting legacy vulnerabilities.

threat_actors.name: Prometheus
threat_actors.description: A ransomware family observed actively targeting the financial services sector.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploit legacy vulnerabilities in unpatched systems, such as Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) in Java-based applications common in financial environments.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups like REvil and Prometheus encrypt data to cause operational disruption and for financial extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploit exposed Telnet services, such as the GoodTech Telnet Server Buffer Overflow, often found on older networking equipment.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: Post-exploitation activity after a successful Log4Shell attack involves the execution of processes like powershell.exe and wmic.exe by compromised Java application servers.

malware.name: REvil
malware.description: A ransomware family actively used in attacks against the financial services sector.

malware.name: Prometheus
malware.description: A ransomware family actively used in attacks against the financial services sector.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used as a post-exploitation tool after a successful Log4Shell attack, often executed by compromised Java application servers.

tools.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
tools.description: The wmic.exe utility is used as a post-exploitation tool after a successful Log4Shell attack, often executed by compromised Java application servers.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2021-44228
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in the Apache Log4j library, referred to as Log4Shell, that continues to be exploited by attackers targeting unpatched Java-based applications in the financial sector.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2014-0160
vulnerabilities.description: A decade-old vulnerability in OpenSSL, known as Heartbleed, that is still actively exploited to steal sensitive data from memory, including private keys and user credentials.

vulnerabilities.name: GoodTech Telnet Server Buffer Overflow
vulnerabilities.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability in the GoodTech Telnet Server that is actively being scanned for and exploited by attackers, targeting older networking equipment and embedded systems.

identities.name: Financial Services Industry
identities.description: The primary target of cyberattacks, experiencing more than double the average attack volume of all other sectors combined, according to a SonicWall report.

indicators.name: Suspicious External Telnet Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious External Telnet Traffic
id: 2f8e1c9a-4b0d-4f3c-8a1b-9e0d2c7b6a5f
status: experimental
description: Detects any network traffic on TCP port 23 (Telnet), which is an insecure protocol and highly suspicious when seen from or to external networks.
references:
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260708sf00032/sonicwall-research-finds-financial-services-running-overdrawn-on-cyber-defenses-as-attack-intensity-outpaces-every-other-tracked-industry
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1210
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    dst_port: 23
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic on TCP port 23 (Telnet). The report notes that any Telnet traffic to or from external networks should be considered highly suspicious.

indicators.name: Log4Shell Exploitation Attempt Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: Log4Shell Exploitation Attempt Pattern
id: 8c9d0e1f-2a3b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects strings associated with Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) exploitation attempts in logs.
references:
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260708sf00032/sonicwall-research-finds-financial-services-running-overdrawn-on-cyber-defenses-as-attack-intensity-outpaces-every-other-tracked-industry
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    sc-uri-query|contains:
      - 'jndi:ldap://'
      - 'jndi:rmi://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects common Log4Shell exploitation patterns such as 'jndi:ldap://' and 'jndi:rmi://' in application and system logs.

indicators.name: Suspicious Process Execution by Java Application
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Process Execution by Java Application
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell or WMIC by common Java application server processes, which can be a post-exploitation step after a successful Log4Shell attack.
references:
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260708sf00032/sonicwall-research-finds-financial-services-running-overdrawn-on-cyber-defenses-as-attack-intensity-outpaces-every-other-tracked-industry
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\jboss.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the execution of powershell.exe or wmic.exe by Java application servers (e.g., Tomcat, JBoss), a potential post-exploitation step for Log4Shell.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Process Execution by Java Application
